an3.65:1.2: At one time, the *Bhagavā*, wandering on tour among the Kosalans together with a large *saṅgha* of *bhikkhus* [*bhikkhusaṅghena*], arrived at the town of the Kālāmās named Kesamutta.
an3.65:1.4: “Sirs, the ascetic Gotama, the Sakyan son who went forth from a Sakyan family, has arrived at Kesamutta.
an3.65:1.5: And concerning Master Gotama, this good report has arisen:
an3.65:1.7: it is good indeed to see such *arahants*.’”
an3.65:11.1: “A hateful person, Kālāmās, overcome by hatred, with mind possessed, kills living beings, takes what is not given, goes to another’s partner, speaks falsely, and encourages others likewise, and that is for his long-term harm and *dukkha*.”
an3.65:13.2: When delusion arises within a person, does it arise for benefit or for harm?”
an3.65:15.1: “A deluded person, Kālāmās, overcome by delusion, with mind possessed, kills living beings, takes what is not given, goes to another’s partner, speaks falsely, and encourages others likewise, and that is for his long-term harm and *dukkha*.”
an3.65:17.1: “What do you think, Kālāmās? Are these *dhammas* skillful or unskillful?”
an3.65:2.1: Then the Kālāmās of Kesamutta went to the *Bhagavā*. Having gone, some paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side; some exchanged greetings with the *Bhagavā*, and after exchanging courteous and amiable talk, sat down to one side; some raised their joined palms toward the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side; some announced their name and clan and sat down to one side; some sat down to one side in silence. Seated to one side, those Kālāmās of Kesamutta said this to the *Bhagavā*:
an3.65:21.1: “Criticized by the wise or praised by the wise?”
an3.65:23.1: “When adopted and practiced, do they lead to harm and *dukkha*, or not?
an3.65:23.2: Or how does it seem to you in this matter?”
an3.65:24.1: “When adopted and practiced, bhante, they lead to harm and *dukkha*.
an3.65:24.2: That is how it seems to us in this matter.”
an3.65:25.1: “So, Kālāmās, what we said to you was this:
an3.65:25.3: Do not go by oral transmission, by lineage, by report, by a collection of texts, by logic, by inference, by reflection on appearances, by acceptance of a view after pondering it, by seeming competence, or because “The ascetic is our teacher.”
an3.65:25.5: “These *dhammas* are unskillful, these *dhammas* are blameworthy, these *dhammas* are criticized by the wise, these *dhammas*, when adopted and practiced, lead to harm and *dukkha*,” then, Kālāmās, you should abandon them’—
an3.65:25.6: it was in reference to this that it was said.
an3.65:26.1: Come, Kālāmās, do not go by oral transmission, by lineage, by report, by a collection of texts, by logic, by inference, by reflection on appearances, by acceptance of a view after pondering it, by seeming competence, or because ‘The ascetic is our teacher.’
an3.65:26.3: ‘These *dhammas* are skillful, these *dhammas* are blameless, these *dhammas* are praised by the wise, these *dhammas*, when adopted and practiced, lead to benefit and *sukha*,’ then, Kālāmās, you should enter upon them and dwell in them.
an3.65:27.2: When non-greed arises within a person, does it arise for benefit or for harm?”
an3.65:29.1: “A person free from greed, Kālāmās, not overcome by greed, with mind unpossessed, does not kill living beings, does not take what is not given, does not go to another’s partner, does not speak falsely, and does not encourage others likewise, and that is for his long-term benefit and *sukha*.”
an3.65:3.1: “There are, bhante, some ascetics and brahmins who come to Kesamutta.
an3.65:3.2: They illuminate and glorify only their own doctrine, but they attack, scorn, despise, and disparage the doctrines of others.
an3.65:3.3: And there are, bhante, some other ascetics and brahmins who come to Kesamutta.
an3.65:3.4: They too illuminate and glorify only their own doctrine, but they attack, scorn, despise, and disparage the doctrines of others.
an3.65:3.5: For us, bhante, there is indeed doubt and uncertainty about them:
an3.65:3.6: ‘Which of these venerable ascetics and brahmins speaks truth, and which speaks falsehood?’”
an3.65:3.7: “It is fitting for you to doubt, Kālāmās; it is fitting for you to be uncertain.
an3.65:3.8: For uncertainty has arisen in you about a doubtful matter.
an3.65:31.2: When non-hatred arises within a person, does it arise …
an3.65:31.3: When non-delusion arises within a person, does it arise …
an3.65:33.1: “What do you think, Kālāmās? Are these *dhammas* skillful or unskillful?”
an3.65:37.1: “Criticized by the wise or praised by the wise?”
an3.65:39.1: “When adopted and practiced, do they lead to benefit and *sukha*, or not?
an3.65:39.2: Or how does it seem to you in this matter?”
an3.65:4.1: Come, Kālāmās, do not go by oral transmission, by lineage, by report, by a collection of texts, by logic, by inference, by reflection on appearances, by acceptance of a view after pondering it, by seeming competence, or because ‘The ascetic is our teacher.’
an3.65:4.3: ‘These *dhammas* are unskillful, these *dhammas* are blameworthy, these *dhammas* are criticized by the wise, these *dhammas*, when adopted and practiced, lead to harm and *dukkha*,’ then, Kālāmās, you should abandon them.
an3.65:40.1: “When adopted and practiced, bhante, they lead to benefit and *sukha*.
an3.65:40.2: That is how it seems to us in this matter.”
an3.65:41.1: “So, Kālāmās, what we said to you was this:
an3.65:41.3: Do not go by oral transmission, by lineage, by report, by a collection of texts, by logic, by inference, by reflection on appearances, by acceptance of a view after pondering it, by seeming competence, or because “The ascetic is our teacher.”
an3.65:41.5: these *dhammas* are skillful, these *dhammas* are blameless, these *dhammas* are praised by the wise, these *dhammas*, when adopted and practiced, lead to benefit and *sukha*—then, Kālāmās, you should enter upon them and dwell in them’—
an3.65:41.6: it was in reference to this that it was said.
an3.65:42.1: That noble disciple, Kālāmās, being thus free from covetousness, free from ill will, unconfused, fully aware [*sampajāno*], with *sati* [*patissato*], dwells pervading one direction with a mind accompanied by goodwill [*mettā*], likewise the second, likewise the third, likewise the fourth. So above, below, across, everywhere, and in every respect, he dwells pervading the whole world with a mind accompanied by goodwill [*mettā*], abundant, expansive, measureless, free from enmity, free from ill will.
an3.65:42.2: With a mind accompanied by compassion [*karuṇā*] …
an3.65:42.3: with a mind accompanied by gladness [*muditā*] …
an3.65:42.4: with a mind accompanied by equanimity [*upekkhā*], he dwells pervading one direction, likewise the second, likewise the third, likewise the fourth. So above, below, across, everywhere, and in every respect, he dwells pervading the whole world with a mind accompanied by equanimity [*upekkhā*], abundant, expansive, measureless, free from enmity, free from ill will.
an3.65:43.1: That noble disciple, Kālāmās, with a mind thus free from enmity, thus free from ill will, thus uncorrupted, thus purified—
an3.65:43.2: gains four assurances in the here and now.
an3.65:43.3: ‘Now, if there is another world, if there is fruit and result of good and bad *kammas*, then with the breakup of the body, after death, I will be reborn in a good destination, a heavenly world’—this is the first assurance he gains.
an3.65:44.1: ‘But if there is no other world, if there is no fruit and result of good and bad *kammas*, then here in this very life I keep myself free from enmity, free from ill will, untroubled, and happy’—this is the second assurance he gains.
an3.65:45.1: ‘And if evil is done by one who does evil, still I do not intend evil toward anyone.
an3.65:45.2: Since I am not doing evil *kamma*, from where could *dukkha* touch me?’—this is the third assurance he gains.
an3.65:46.1: ‘And if evil is not done by one who does evil, then in either case I see myself as pure’—this is the fourth assurance he gains.
an3.65:47.1: That noble disciple, Kālāmās, with a mind thus free from enmity, thus free from ill will, thus uncorrupted, thus purified—
an3.65:47.2: gains these four assurances in the here and now.”
an3.65:48.1: “So it is, *Bhagavā*, so it is, Well-Gone One.
an3.65:48.2: That noble disciple, bhante, with a mind thus free from enmity, thus free from ill will, thus uncorrupted, thus purified—
an3.65:48.3: gains four assurances in the here and now.
an3.65:48.4: ‘Now, if there is another world, if there is fruit and result of good and bad *kammas*, then with the breakup of the body, after death, I will be reborn in a good destination, a heavenly world’—this is the first assurance he gains.
an3.65:49.1: ‘But if there is no other world, if there is no fruit and result of good and bad *kammas*, then here in this very life I keep myself free from enmity, free from ill will, untroubled, and happy’—this is the second assurance he gains.
an3.65:5.2: When greed arises within a person, does it arise for benefit or for harm?”
an3.65:50.1: ‘And if evil is done by one who does evil, still I do not intend evil toward anyone; since I am not doing evil *kamma*, from where could *dukkha* touch me?’—this is the third assurance he gains.
an3.65:51.1: ‘And if evil is not done by one who does evil, then in either case I see myself as pure’—this is the fourth assurance he gains.
an3.65:52.1: That noble disciple, bhante, with a mind thus free from enmity, thus free from ill will, thus uncorrupted, thus purified—
an3.65:52.2: gains these four assurances in the here and now.
an3.65:53.2: We go for refuge, bhante, to the *Bhagavā*, to the *dhamma*, and to the *saṅgha* of *bhikkhus* [*bhikkhusaṅghañca*].
an3.65:53.3: May the *Bhagavā* remember us as lay followers who have gone for refuge from this day for life.”
an3.65:7.1: “A greedy person, Kālāmās, overcome by greed, with mind possessed, kills living beings, takes what is not given, goes to another’s partner, speaks falsely, and encourages others likewise, and that is for his long-term harm and *dukkha*.”
an3.65:9.2: When hatred arises within a person, does it arise for benefit or for harm?”
